sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः |
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ||
transliteration:
indriyāṇi parāṇy āhur indriyebhyaḥ paraṁ manaḥ |
manasas tu parā buddhir yo buddheḥ paratas tu saḥ ||
literal_meaning: The senses are said to be superior to the body; higher than the senses is the mind; beyond the mind is the intellect; and the one beyond even the intellect is the soul.
contextual_meaning: Krishna presents a vertical map of consciousness: body → senses → mind → intellect → soul. Each layer can override the one below, but only the soul can override all. This hierarchy is the basis for self-regulation: by identifying with the soul rather than the lower layers, one can redirect the entire system toward purposeful action (dharma) instead of impulsive reaction.
psychological_state: Arjuna shifts from sensory overwhelm (battlefield stimuli) to intellectual clarity (hierarchy model) and finally to soul-level agency, reducing paralysis and restoring executive control.
trigger_scenario: A professional feels hijacked by notifications, cravings, and anxious thoughts. Realizing these are just layers—not identity—she can step into the observer (soul) position and choose values-based action.
anchor_text: You are the soul, master of every layer beneath you.
